topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the stark finality of a closing door. My fingers hovered over the screen, the cursor blinking mockingly on the subject line: "Scholarship Application Update." I reread the rejection letter three times, the words swimming together into an indistinct blur. A coldness seeped into my chest, a slow, heavy pressure that made my breath catch in my throat. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small confines of my dorm room, the cheap carpet muffling the sound of my quick, agitated steps. Every shadow in the room seemed to stretch and distort, taking on a menacing shape. I pressed my palms against my eyes, trying to physically block out the harsh reality of the rejection, but the feeling persisted—a tremor running through my hands that made gripping my pen feel impossible.

[story 2]

I kept glancing at the clock on my desk, the seconds dragging with agonizing slowness. The silence of the apartment was oppressive, amplifying the frantic drumming in my ears. I picked up the rejection letter again, turning the glossy paper over and over in my hands until the edges felt frayed. My jaw tightened until a dull ache radiated across my cheeks. I started cleaning, obsessively wiping down the already spotless surfaces, the repetitive motion a desperate attempt to impose order on the swirling chaos inside my head. When I finally dropped the paper onto the floor, the sound seemed deafening. I walked to the window, staring out at the indifferent city lights, feeling a strange detachment, as if I were watching a scene from a movie where I was the only one who knew the ending was devastatingly wrong.

[story 3]

"No." The word echoed in the empty hallway of the library. I hadn't even expected the email to carry such a weight. My stomach clenched, a tight, hard knot that refused to loosen. I walked back to my seat, my movements jerky and uncoordinated. I stared blankly at my textbook, the equations on the page suddenly meaningless, a jumble of ink mocking my efforts. My gaze darted around the room, cataloging every misplaced object, searching for some tangible clue that could explain this inexplicable failure. A bead of sweat traced a cold path down my temple. I felt a profound instability, as if the ground beneath my feet had suddenly shifted, leaving me precarious and utterly unsure of the next step.
